title: Tangy Silky Canadian Lime Bisquettes
description: A tangy, silky crowd-pleaser featuring lime and a hint of Canadian twist, perfect for broiling in the comfort of your own home.

ingredients.name: Cauliflower florets
ingredients.notes: Medium-sized head of cauliflower, broken into florets.

ingredients.name: Lime juice
ingredients.notes: Freshly squeezed lime juice for optimal flavor.

ingredients.name: Maple syrup
ingredients.notes: A hint of sweetness to balance the tanginess.

ingredients.name: Ginger powder
ingredients.notes: Adds a warm, spicy depth to the dish.

ingredients.name: Garlic powder
ingredients.notes: Complementing the ginger with a mellow, savory flavor.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: A pinch of salt to bring out the natural flavors.

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: A sprinkle of black pepper for added depth.

ingredients.name: Avocado oil
ingredients.notes: Healthy oil for broiling and adding moisture.

ingredients.name: Fresh cilantro
ingredients.notes: Garnish with chopped cilantro for a pop of color and freshness.

ingredients.name: Lime zest
ingredients.notes: Grated lime zest for added citrusy aroma.

ingredients.name: Balsamic vinegar
ingredients.notes: A drizzle of balsamic vinegar for a tangy, sweet contrast.

ingredients.name: Shiitake mushrooms
ingredients.notes: Sliced mushrooms for added texture and umami flavor.

instructions: Preheat your broiler to high heat.
Line a baking sheet with parchment paper, leaving enough space for the bisquettes to spread out.
Toss the cauliflower florets with avocado oil, salt, black pepper, and a pinch of garlic powder until they're evenly coated.
Arrange the cauliflower mixture on the prepared baking sheet, leaving a little space between each floret.
In a small bowl, whisk together lime juice, maple syrup, ginger powder, and a pinch of salt.
Brush the mixture evenly over the cauliflower florets, making sure to get some lime juice on each piece.
Sprinkle the chopped cilantro and grated lime zest over the cauliflower.
Drizzle the balsamic vinegar over the cauliflower, but be careful not to make it too soggy.
Broil the cauliflower for 5-7 minutes, or until it's tender and slightly caramelized.
Meanwhile, toss the sliced shiitake mushrooms with a pinch of garlic powder and a sprinkle of salt.
Add the mushrooms to the baking sheet with the cauliflower and broil for another 2-3 minutes, or until they're tender and fragrant.
Remove the bisquettes from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes.
Serve warm, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
Repeat the process with the remaining ingredients, if needed.
